title: Podman doesn't connect behind corporate proxy
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

podman doesn't connect behind corporate proxy

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
```
root@podmanhost01:/home/domain/user1# echo $http_proxy
http://domain\user1:userpass@proxy.company.com:8080
root@podmanhost01:/home/domain/user1# echo $https_proxy
http://domain\user1:userpass@proxy.company.com:8080
root@podmanhost01:/home/domain/user1# podman pull mariadb
```
**Describe the results you received:**
```
root@podmanhost01:/home/domain/user1# podman pull mariadb
Trying to pull docker.io/library/mariadb...
  Get "https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/": proxyconnect tcp: dial tcp: lookup http: Temporary failure in name resolution
Trying to pull quay.io/mariadb...
  Get "https://quay.io/v2/": proxyconnect tcp: dial tcp: lookup http: Temporary failure in name resolution
Error: unable to pull mariadb: 2 errors occurred:
        * Error initializing source docker://mariadb:latest: error pinging docker registry registry-1.docker.io: Get "https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/": proxyconnect tcp: dial tcp: lookup http: Temporary failure in name resolution
        * Error initializing source docker://quay.io/mariadb:latest: error pinging docker registry quay.io: Get "https://quay.io/v2/": proxyconnect tcp: dial tcp: lookup http: Temporary failure in name resolution
```
**Describe the results you expected:**

Not an error :)

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Docker works:
```
root@podmanhost01:/home/domain/user1# docker pull mariadb
Using default tag: latest
latest: Pulling from library/mariadb

Status: Downloaded newer image for mariadb:latest
docker.io/library/mariadb:latest
```

```

Built:        Thu Jan  1 02:00:00 1970

```


comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

If this is true it means that their is a bug in golang itself, since we are using the golang bindings for connecting to web services, which are supposed to follow these flags.

@vrothberg WDYT?

A Go bug would surprise me as it would impact many people at once.

@danny2001k, could you share `/etc/containers/containers.conf` and try setting the `env=["http_proxy=..."]` in the `[engines]` table? Does that work? `containers.conf` is the only offender I can imagine in our code base at the moment.

TL;DR: “be liberal in what you accept” bites again, combined with a lazy implementation in the standard library.  The `\` must be percent-encoded; a Go standard library bug might be worth filing.

---

> ```
> root@podmanhost01:/home/domain/user1# echo $http_proxy
> http://domain\user1:userpass@proxy.company.com:8080
> root@podmanhost01:/home/domain/user1# echo $https_proxy
> http://domain\user1:userpass@proxy.company.com:8080
> root@podmanhost01:/home/domain/user1# podman pull mariadb
> ```

> proxyconnect tcp:

The configuration was recognized as a proxy configuration, the code is trying to contact the proxy.

> dial tcp: lookup http: Temporary failure in name resolution

The code has decided that the proxy hostname is `http`.

---

Note:
```go
func main() {
	in := `http://domain\user1:userpass@proxy.company.com:8080`
	url, err := url.Parse(in)
	fmt.Printf("%v\n", err)
}
```
results in a `parse "http://domain\\user1:userpass@proxy.company.com:8080": net/url: invalid userinfo` because `\` is not a valid character in that part of the URL.

https://golang.org/pkg/net/http/#ProxyFromEnvironment says
> The environment values may be either a complete URL or a "host[:port]", in which case the "http" scheme is assumed. An error is returned if the value is a different form.

but what the function _actually_ does is not parse a host[:port] but
```go
                // proxy was bogus. Try prepending "http://" to it and
                // see if that parses correctly. If not, we fall
                // through and complain about the original one.
                if proxyURL, err := url.Parse("http://" + proxy); err == nil {
```
and, well,
```go
func main() {
	in := `http://http://domain\user1:userpass@proxy.company.com:8080`
	url, err := url.Parse(in)
	fmt.Printf("%#v %#v\n", url, err)
}
```
results in
```
&url.URL{Scheme:"http", Opaque:"", User:(*url.Userinfo)(nil), Host:"http:", Path:"//domain\\user1:userpass@proxy.company.com:8080", RawPath:"//domain\\user1:userpass@proxy.company.com:8080", ForceQuery:false, RawQuery:"", Fragment:"", RawFragment:""} <nil>
```
which matches the observed error with `Host:"http:"`.

---

I didn’t look into why it works with Docker. As a general principle, I’m not a fan of individual applications overriding standard library behavior. Either change the configuration, or change the standard library; a single change will make many applications functional. Every single application fixing up the standard library behavior (and having to _know_ it needs fixing up) does not scale at all.

Same problem, I can't pull registries behind corporate proxy.

```
[agrandville@sv1396 containers]$ sudo podman run hello-world
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/hello-world...
  Get https://registry.access.redhat.com/v2/: proxyconnect tcp: EOF
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/hello-world...
  Get https://registry.fedoraproject.org/v2/: proxyconnect tcp: EOF
Trying to pull registry.centos.org/hello-world...
  Get https://registry.centos.org/v2/: proxyconnect tcp: EOF

Trying to pull docker.io/library/hello-world...
  Get https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/: proxyconnect tcp: EOF
Error: unable to pull hello-world: 4 errors occurred:
        * Error initializing source docker://registry.access.redhat.com/hello-world:latest: error pinging docker registry registry.access.redhat.com: Get https://registry.access.redhat.com/v2/: proxyconnect tcp: EOF
        * Error initializing source docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/hello-world:latest: error pinging docker registry registry.fedoraproject.org: Get https://registry.fedoraproject.org/v2/: proxyconnect tcp: EOF
        * Error initializing source docker://registry.centos.org/hello-world:latest: error pinging docker registry registry.centos.org: Get https://registry.centos.org/v2/: proxyconnect tcp: EOF
        * Error initializing source docker://hello-world:latest: error pinging docker registry registry-1.docker.io: Get https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/: proxyconnect tcp: EOF

```

A tcpdump shows the problem

CUrl gracefull proxy request with CONNECT

```
1	17:20:38,415729	192.168.101.159	192.168.69.12	TCP	66	38130 → 3128 [SYN] Seq=0 Win=29200 Len=0 MSS=1460 SACK_PERM=1 WS=128
2	17:20:38,416535	192.168.69.12	192.168.101.159	TCP	62	3128 → 38130 [SYN, ACK] Seq=0 Ack=1 Win=65535 Len=0 MSS=1460 SACK_PERM=1
3	17:20:38,416551	192.168.101.159	192.168.69.12	TCP	54	38130 → 3128 [ACK] Seq=1 Ack=1 Win=29200 Len=0
4	17:20:38,416630	192.168.101.159	192.168.69.12	HTTP	198	CONNECT registry.access.redhat.com:443 HTTP/1.1 
5	17:20:38,455361	192.168.69.12	192.168.101.159	HTTP	93	HTTP/1.1 200 Connection established 
6	17:20:38,455374	192.168.101.159	192.168.69.12	TCP	54	38130 → 3128 [ACK] Seq=145 Ack=40 Win=29200 Len=0
7	17:20:38,612624	192.168.101.159	192.168.69.12	TLSv1.2	262	Client Hello
8	17:20:38,665385	192.168.69.12	192.168.101.159	TLSv1.2	1414	Server Hello
9	17:20:38,665412	192.168.101.159	192.168.69.12	TCP	54	38130 → 3128 [ACK] Seq=353 Ack=1400 Win=31280 Len=0
10	17:20:38,665423	192.168.69.12	192.168.101.159	TCP	154	3128 → 38130 [PSH, ACK] Seq=1400 Ack=353 Win=65535 Len=100 [TCP segment of a reassembled PDU]
...
```

podman without CONNECT 

```
8	16:40:39,995732	192.168.101.159	192.168.69.12	TCP	66	38084 → 3128 [SYN] Seq=0 Win=29200 Len=0 MSS=1460 SACK_PERM=1 WS=128
10	16:40:39,996939	192.168.69.12	192.168.101.159	TCP	62	3128 → 38084 [SYN, ACK] Seq=0 Ack=1 Win=65535 Len=0 MSS=1460 SACK_PERM=1
11	16:40:39,996953	192.168.101.159	192.168.69.12	TCP	54	38084 → 3128 [ACK] Seq=1 Ack=1 Win=29200 Len=0
12	16:40:39,997056	192.168.101.159	192.168.69.12	TLSv1	250	Client Hello
13	16:40:39,997929	192.168.69.12	192.168.101.159	TCP	60	3128 → 38084 [FIN, ACK] Seq=1 Ack=197 Win=65535 Len=0
14	16:40:39,997975	192.168.101.159	192.168.69.12	TCP	54	38084 → 3128 [FIN, ACK] Seq=197 Ack=2 Win=29200 Len=0
15	16:40:39,998897	192.168.69.12	192.168.101.159	TCP	60	3128 → 38084 [ACK] Seq=2 Ack=198 Win=65534 Len=0
EOF
```
@agrandville That’s certainly a different root cause (no `dial tcp: lookup http`), please file a separate report.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since this is in the standard library, it is not something we can fix.  Should we close this?
@agrandville @mtrmac @vrothberg Where do we stand on this one?
AFAICT the original is user error + missing syntax checking in the standard library.
Therefore this is not something Podman can fix, Closing.  Reopen if I am mistaken.

I've got the same issue, except my proxy does not require a username and password.
I'm trying to follow the comments here. e.g. look at `/etc/containers/containers.conf`. But there is no `/etc/containers` directory on my machine.
You need to create it and then podman will read the containers.conf file from there.
@mdavis-xyz Please file new reports instead of piling on to closed ones if they don’t actually match the situation. It’s just going to be lost here.
